column1:
Using the profile you extracted in Part A, list the 3 eligible loads with the highest effective rate per mile = price ÷ (deadhead-to-origin + loaded miles + deadhead-home).

Filter by equipment, weight, and minimum rate BEFORE ranking.

See README.md in the GitHub repo. Summary: filtered on equipment (Hotshot/Gooseneck), weight capacity (16,500 lb, inferred), and min rate ($2.00/mi). L06 (missing price) and L07 (missing destination) excluded, not guessed. Rejected high-paying load: L08 ($1,700, highest price on board) — passes filters but its long deadhead-home from McAllen back to San Antonio drops it to 2.480/mi, behind L03's 3.098/mi despite L03's lower $1,500 price.
